lecture_context.lecture_title: CS224N Lecture 10: Post-training for Large Language Models

unit.name: Limitations of supervised fine-tuning
unit.description: Explains why SFT alone is insufficient and motivates preference optimization.
unit.summary: The instructor summarizes instruction fine-tuning as simple, then lists why it is not enough [ts=2043s] [ts=2068s]. It depends on expensive human demonstrations, especially for specialized questions that may require high expertise [ts=2076s] [ts=2098s]. Many open-ended or creative tasks do not have a single correct answer [ts=2116s] [ts=2125s]. Token-level language modeling loss also treats mistakes too uniformly: calling Avatar an adventure show and calling it a musical can be penalized similarly despite very different semantic severity [ts=2135s] [ts=2164s]. Another issue is that human-written answers may become a bottleneck as models generate better answers than labelers [ts=2169s] [ts=2192s]. The main conceptual problem is objective mismatch: the desired goal is outputs humans prefer, but SFT still performs next-token prediction on curated data [ts=2215s] [ts=2238s].

key_points.text: Collecting expert demonstrations is expensive for complex or specialized tasks.
Creative and open-ended tasks may not have one exact correct answer.
Token-level loss can penalize semantically mild and severe mistakes equally.
Human-generated answers can cap quality when models become stronger than labelers.
SFT still optimizes next-token prediction rather than human preference directly.

kp_catalog.name: Limitations of supervised fine-tuning
kp_catalog.description: SFT limitations including expensive demonstrations, open-ended outputs, token-loss mismatch, and human quality bottlenecks.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because limitations of supervised fine-tuning clarifies sFT limitations including expensive demonstrations, open-ended outputs, token-loss mismatch, and human quality bottlenecks, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: RLHF preference optimization
kp_catalog.description: The RLHF reward-maximization objective with KL regularization and Bradley-Terry-style pairwise rewards. Optimizing language models for learned human preference rewards over model-generated outputs.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because rLHF preference optimization formalizes how the RLHF reward-maximization objective with KL regularization and Bradley-Terry-style pairwise rewards, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
